mnemonics: P.E.A.R.L.S. (Polynomials: Efficiently Added, Represented, and Linked for Storage) to remember the key concepts of representation, addition, and storage.
keyPoints: A polynomial is a mathematical expression of one or more terms with non-negative integer exponents.
The two main representations are the Array Representation (dense form) and the Linked List Representation (sparse form).
The core application is polynomial addition, where coefficients of like terms (same exponent) are summed.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize the coefficient array elements to zero in the array representation, leading to garbage values.
In linked list representation, failing to maintain the list in sorted order (by exponent) makes addition inefficient and complex.
Incorrectly handling the insertion of a new node when the resulting coefficient from addition becomes zero (the node should be freed, not added).
